anaphoric_renaming.note: haiku's two findings ('a full round', 'one of some note') read the placeholder-noun class broadly; GPT-5.6 explicitly cleared the class ('uses of thing ... do not vaguely rename the headword'); reported unedited as measured

staged_observers.note: haiku flags 'the eye' / 'organ of sight' / 'the naked eye' as staged observers; GPT-5.6 explicitly cleared the same entries ('references to the eye in 27 and 67 describe visual perception as part of the meaning; they do not invent generic onlookers'); direct inter-family contradiction on the same bytes, reported unedited

non_definitional_material.note: haiku's eight findings treat standard lexicographic function/context clauses (e.g. box 'used for holding or carrying goods', condolence 'addressed to the bereaved') as non-definitional; GPT-5.6's single finding is the permanence clause of entry 62 (kind)

internal_consistency_observations: haiku lists entry 31 (day) among the MOST natural entries and simultaneously among the LEAST natural entries in the same answer
the two families contradict each other outright on staged observers (haiku 4 findings, GPT-5.6 explicit none on the same entries)

verdict.part1_defect_audit: NOT clean: GPT-5.6 one non-definitional finding (entry 62 permanence clause); Haiku findings in all three hard classes (2 anaphoric, 4 staged-observer, 8 non-definitional), many arguably class misapplications per the notes above, but the gate is blind and the counts stand as MEASURED
verdict.part2_naturalness_floor: NOT met by EITHER family: GPT-5.6 4/10 (v3: 8/10), Haiku 3/10 (v3: 4/10)
verdict.why: the frame-variation retry eliminated the v3 set-level template monotony (neither judge reports rigid parallelism or semicolon-paraphrase templates; entry 62 kind, the v3 consensus-worst, draws zero register findings) but the varied framing itself reads to both families as an 'old-fashioned pseudo-lexicographic voice' (GPT-5.6) / 'theatrically formal' register (Haiku); the failure mode INVERTED from too-uniform (v3) to too-mannered (v4), and the GPT-5.6 score moved 8 -> 4 on wording changes alone
verdict.instrument_observation: across v3 and v4 the two-family instrument is unstable at set level on the same underlying content: v3 measured 8/4, v4 measures 4/3, with mutually contradictory set-level diagnoses; per ASM-0706 this is evidence relevant to the maintainer's option (B) (accept-as-control) or an instrument re-ruling, not something this session may adjudicate
verdict.disposition: freeze precondition NOT met by v4; both stores (v3, v4) and both gate readouts go to the maintainer under issue 17 with the sample-responses evidence; no freeze action taken by this session
